findings.evidence: Gradings.lean:48,65,85 mark maslovO_def/maslovX_def/alexander_def @[simp]; the new defs at GradingInteger.lean:76,81,123 have no corresponding restatement.
findings.fix: Add @[simp] theorems maslovOℤ_def, maslovXℤ_def, alexanderTwoℤ_def stating each definition's RHS (proof `rfl`), matching the existing *_def pattern.
findings.issue: maslovOℤ, maslovXℤ (line 81) and alexanderTwoℤ (line 123) have no characteristic *_def lemma. Every grading-level definition in Gradings.lean carries one (maslovO_def, maslovX_def, alexander_def, all @[simp]); these break that convention, so a consumer cannot restate or rewrite the integer formula without `unfold`.

findings.evidence: JFunction.lean:350 `@[simp] theorem J_singleton_self`, :213/:218 `@[simp] J_empty_left/right`; J_self/JNum_self are the analogous self-pairing facts but unannotated.
findings.fix: Mark J_self @[simp] (and consider @[grind =]); annotate JNum_self similarly so JNum s s and J s s reduce on the integrality lane.
findings.issue: J_self (J s s = I s s) is a self-pairing normal-form reduction in the same family as J_singleton_self, J_empty_left/right, JDiff_self_left/right — all @[simp] in JFunction.lean — yet carries no @[simp]/@[grind]; JNum_self (line 55) likewise. Self-pairings will not normalize automatically downstream.

summary: Exposure is correctly minimal (no @[expose], bodies hidden) and the rational-cast characteristic lemmas are present, but the three new definitions lack the @[simp] *_def restatements that every other grading-level def carries, and the self-pairing reductions J_self/JNum_self lack the @[simp] annotation their siblings have.
